channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Supreme Court's Push to Erode Civil Rights Sparks Legal Expert Concerns
item.description: A legal expert has criticized the U.S. Supreme Court's recent actions as 'deeply flawed' and indicative of a broader effort to undermine constitutional rights. During an interview with political comme...

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 quarter-final matchups: Full guide to the eight teams
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup quarter-finals feature eight teams competing for a spot in the semi-finals. Key matches include France vs Morocco, Spain vs Belgium, Norway vs England, and Argentina vs Switzer...

item.title: Israel Shares Intelligence on Iranian Plot to Assassinate Trump with U.S.
item.description: Sources report that Israel shared intelligence with the U.S. regarding an Iranian plot to assassinate former President Donald Trump. The information, according to the sources, highlights ongoing tensi...

item.title: Two New ETFs Exclude Elon Musk's Companies to Avoid Investment Risks
item.description: Two newly registered ETFs, Nasdaq-100 Ex-Elon Enterprises ETF (QQNE) and S&P 500 Ex-Elon Enterprises ETF (SPNE), have been created by Subversive Capital to exclude companies associated with Elon Musk....

item.title: Israeli strikes in Gaza kill six, ceasefire holds but violence continues
item.description: Israeli drone strikes and gunfire in Gaza have killed six people, according to the civil defence agency and health officials. The attacks occurred during a ceasefire in the Israel-Hamas war, but viole...

item.title: South Africa's Manufacturing Sector Faces Sharp Decline in May 2026
item.description: Statistics South Africa reported a 4.3% year-on-year decline in manufacturing production for May 2026, worse than economists' expectations. Key sectors contributing to the decline include food and bev...

item.title: Climate change linked to increased Himalayan forest fires
item.description: A study reveals that rising temperatures are significantly contributing to more frequent forest fires in the Himalayas, despite human activity remaining a major cause. Researchers from the Forest Rese...

item.title: Think tank proposes cap on political donations to rebuild trust in UK democracy
item.description: A new report by the Institute for Public Policy Research (IPPR) argues that capping private political donations could restore trust in British democracy. The report calls for a £100,000 annual limit o...

item.title: Tinubu's Admin Reviews National Security Strategy Amid Rising Threats
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu chaired a high-level security meeting at the Presidential Villa in Abuja to review the nation's security architecture and address threats like terrorism and banditry. Senio...

item.title: Gauteng government launches coordinated efforts to address Lesedi municipality's water and financial crisis
item.description: The Gauteng Provincial Government has initiated a coordinated intervention to address ongoing challenges in Lesedi Local Municipality, focusing on restoring financial stability and ensuring reliable w...

item.title: Ancient giant kangaroos survived on New Guinea coast until 6,500 years ago
item.description: New research reveals that Protemnodon tumbuna, a large kangaroo species, survived in northern New Guinea until around 6,500 years ago. This challenges previous assumptions that all megafauna in Austra...

item.title: Persian Gulf hostilities delay oil supply recovery hopes
item.description: Renewed clashes between the United States and Iran in the Persian Gulf have disrupted global oil markets, delaying recovery of crude supply. The conflict, now in its fifth month, has pushed oil prices...

item.title: Susan Collins' Re-Election Strategy Falters After Democratic Challenger's Exit
item.description: Maine Republican Senator Susan Collins' re-election strategy has collapsed following the abrupt withdrawal of Democratic candidate Graham Platner from the Senate race. Platner's exit, prompted by mult...

item.title: Ateneo and DOLE Dispute Over Tab Baldwin's Employment Status
item.description: The Ateneo de Manila University is contesting the Department of Labor and Employment's (DOLE) claim that former men's basketball coach Tab Baldwin is an employee, asserting he is a consultant. Lawyers...

item.title: Funeral Prayers Held for Late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei in Mashhad
item.description: The funeral prayers for assassinated supreme leader Ali Khamenei were offered in Mashhad, Iran, as hundreds of thousands of mourners gathered despite extreme heat. The assassination occurred in late F...

item.title: Nigeria Police Postpone PCRC Zone 13 Election Due to Allegations of Irregularities
item.description: The Nigeria Police Force has postponed the election for the Police Community Relations Committee (PCRC) in Zone 13, originally scheduled for July 8, 2026. The decision comes after allegations that the...

item.title: Defence Minister Orders Immediate Action Against Bandits, Unveils Major Security Equipment
item.description: Nigeria's Defence Minister, Mohammed Badaru Abubakar, has directed troops to engage bandits and terrorists on sight without waiting for superior orders. The directive was given during the unveiling of...

item.title: Trump DOJ Prosecutes Olympian Over Reflecting Pool Damage Amid Corruption Concerns
item.description: U.S. Rep. Jamie Raskin (D-MD) criticized the Trump administration's legal action against David Hearn, an Olympic canoeist accused of damaging the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. Hearn faces a felony...

item.title: Boston Bar wildfires destroy part of B.C. resort as Brunswick complex continues to grow
item.description: The owner of Blue Lake Resort near Boston Bar, B.C., says a significant portion of his property has been destroyed as the Brunswick complex wildfires continue to burn across the Fraser Canyon. Shayne ...

item.title: 23 Individuals Arrested on Incitement Charges in Tanzania
item.description: Police in Tanzania have arrested 23 individuals on charges of incitement, including a prominent religious leader and a local government official serving as a street chairperson. The arrests were made ...
